summary: I was born in Taiwan, a beautiful island where I spent most of my childhood. Growing up there shaped much of who I am, especially in my hometown of Taichung. Taiwan is where my family's cycling factory is located, and it's the place I always think of when I see my family photo.

Taichung, or T'ai-chung as it's sometimes called, is where I was born and raised. It has a great landscape and a suitable temperature, making it a wonderful place to grow up. My parents have owned a cycling factory in the Dajia District of Taichung for over twenty years, which I decided to take over.

After growing up in Taichung, I moved to Taipei, the capital city of Taiwan, to complete my education in economics. It was in Taipei where I finished university before receiving the message from my parents about taking over the family business. Taipei represents an important step in my academic and personal journey.

Japan is one of the main export destinations for my family's cycling factory. Since our business focuses on exporting to Japan, I needed to improve my English to communicate effectively with customers there. This connection to Japan influenced my decision to study English abroad.

Europe is another key market for my family's cycling factory exports. Like Japan, I had to learn English to communicate with European customers. This international aspect of the business motivated me to come to the United States for formal English education.

I came to the United States to improve my English because my skills were very poor, and I needed to communicate with international customers. I enrolled at Century College in Minnesota in July 2014 to get formal university instruction. The U.S. has been a place of both struggle and growth for me as I adapt to a new culture while pursuing my goals.

I enrolled at Century College in Minnesota on July 2, 2014, to improve my English skills. This college became my first step in formal university instruction in the United States. Studying there was challenging at first, but it helped me gain the confidence to communicate and pursue my family business goals.

Minnesota is where I studied at Century College starting in 2014 to improve my English. Living in Minnesota was a big change from Taiwan, and I initially felt frustrated and pressured. However, it became a place where I worked hard to overcome language barriers and cultural differences.

The family photo I brought with me was taken during our Chinese New Year celebration in 2014. This event is very special to us because family time is rare, and we make sure to spend those few days together. The photo reminds me of my family's love and hard work, giving me strength when I feel frustrated far from home.
